key_insight: A unified constitutive and optimization framework enables predictive design of hard-magnetic soft materials by coupling effective shear-modulus modeling with joint control of structure, particle fraction, and magnetization direction.
engineering_relevance: This supports more reliable inverse design of soft robotic and adaptive structures where field-driven deformation must be tuned without contact.
method: Unified 7 effective shear-modulus relations under a common framework, paired with 3 strain-energy density models.
Evaluated 21 constitutive model combinations for hard-magnetic soft materials.
Built a joint optimization scheme for structural density, magnetic particle volume fraction, and remanent magnetization direction across multiple loading scenarios.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports comparison against experimental stress-strain data, with the Mooney shear-modulus relation giving the best overall agreement.
watch_item: Model predictions can be sensitive when magnetic material is placed in regions of large deformation, so constitutive choice and geometric overlap must be handled carefully.
paper_title: Model-Informed Joint Material-Structural Optimization of Hard-Magnetic Soft Materials
